questions.q: Who was Karna's birth mother?
options: Radha
Madri
Gandhari
Kunti
questions.explanation: Kunti bore Karna before her marriage after testing the sage Durvasa's mantra on the sun god.

questions.q: Which god fathered Karna?
options: Surya, the sun god
Indra, the storm god
Agni, the fire god
Vayu, the wind god
questions.explanation: Karna was the son of Surya, born radiant with golden armor and earrings.

questions.q: Who raised Karna after he was found floating on the river?
options: Drona and his wife Kripi
The charioteer Adhiratha and his wife Radha
A fisherman chief and his wife
King Shalya and the queen of Madra
questions.explanation: The charioteer Adhiratha and his wife Radha found the abandoned infant and raised him as their own son.

questions.q: With what was Karna born, marking his divine origin?
options: A jewel set into his forehead
A bow that no other man could lift
Golden armor and earrings fused to his body
A mark of the sun blazing upon his chest
questions.explanation: Karna was born wearing natural golden armor and earrings that made him nearly invulnerable.

questions.q: Who crowned Karna king of Anga at the tournament of princes?
options: Duryodhana
Dhritarashtra
Bhishma
Shakuni
questions.explanation: When Karna was mocked for his charioteer birth, Duryodhana crowned him king of Anga on the spot, winning his lifelong loyalty.

questions.q: To whom did Karna give away his golden armor and earrings?
options: Krishna, who came to test his heart
Surya, who came to reclaim his gifts
A brahmin whose cow he had killed
Indra, disguised as a brahmin beggar
questions.explanation: Indra came disguised as a brahmin to weaken Karna before the war, and Karna cut the armor from his own flesh and gave it.

questions.q: Who killed Karna on the battlefield of Kurukshetra?
options: Bhima
Yudhishthira
Arjuna
Sahadeva
questions.explanation: Arjuna slew Karna on the seventeenth day of the war while Karna's chariot wheel lay trapped in the earth.

questions.q: Karna's unshakable loyalty bound him to which prince?
options: Yudhishthira
Duryodhana
Dushasana
Vikarna
questions.explanation: Karna remained loyal to Duryodhana, who had honored him when the world mocked his birth.

questions.q: For which virtue was Karna famed above all other warriors?
options: His patience
His humility
His honesty
His generosity
questions.explanation: Karna vowed never to refuse anyone who asked of him at his worship of the sun, earning fame as the most generous of men.

questions.q: The name Radheya, often used for Karna, honors whom?
options: Radha, his foster mother
Radha, the beloved of Krishna
The river on which he was found
The city in which he was crowned
questions.explanation: Karna was called Radheya after Radha, the charioteer's wife who lovingly raised him.

questions.q: What was Karna's birth-name, given because he was born bearing golden wealth on his body?
options: Vrishaketu
Vasusena
Vikartana
Sudaman
questions.explanation: The infant was named Vasusena because he was born with the wealth of golden armor and earrings.

questions.q: Why did Parashurama curse Karna, his own student?
options: Karna struck him during a practice duel
Karna stole the secret of the Brahmastra
Karna had hidden his true birth, posing as a brahmin
Karna abandoned his training before its completion
questions.explanation: Parashurama taught only brahmins, and on discovering Karna's deception he cursed his gifted pupil.

questions.q: What incident revealed to Parashurama that Karna was no brahmin?
options: Karna strung a bow that only kshatriyas may touch
Karna refused to beg for alms in the village
Karna's golden earrings began to glow before the guru
Karna silently endured a stinging insect boring into his thigh
questions.explanation: Karna bore the agony without moving so his sleeping guru would not wake, an endurance Parashurama judged beyond any brahmin.

questions.q: What did Parashurama's curse decree?
options: Karna would forget his ultimate weapon at the hour of greatest need
Karna would die before his thirty-third year of life
Karna would never defeat another student of Parashurama
Karna would lose every duel fought after the sun had set
questions.explanation: Parashurama decreed that the weapon-lore Karna had learned by deceit would desert him at the moment he needed it most.

questions.q: Why did a brahmin curse Karna that his chariot wheel would sink in battle?
options: Karna had insulted him at the tournament grounds
Karna had accidentally killed the brahmin's cow
Karna had refused him a gift at his midday worship
Karna had trampled his field during archery practice
questions.explanation: Karna's stray arrow killed a brahmin's cow, and the grieving owner cursed that the earth would swallow his wheel in his final fight.

questions.q: What did Indra grant Karna in return for the armor cut from his flesh?
options: The bow Vijaya and two inexhaustible quivers
Immunity from every celestial weapon
The Vasavi Shakti, an infallible dart usable only once
A chariot drawn by the white horses of heaven
questions.explanation: Shamed by Karna's generosity, Indra gave him the Vasavi Shakti, a dart that could not miss but would return to the god after one use.

questions.q: Against whom was Karna finally forced to spend Indra's one-use dart?
options: Abhimanyu, inside the wheel formation
Bhima, during the duel of the seventeenth day
Arjuna, in their final confrontation
Ghatotkacha, during his night rampage
questions.explanation: On the fourteenth night Ghatotkacha's devastation forced Karna to hurl the Shakti he had saved for Arjuna.

questions.q: What promise did Karna make to Kunti at the riverbank before the war?
options: He would spare four of the Pandavas and fight only Arjuna
He would desert Duryodhana on the first day of battle
He would never aim an arrow at any son of hers
He would reveal his true birth to the world after the war
questions.explanation: Karna promised Kunti that she would always have five sons, for he would kill none of her children except Arjuna.

questions.q: When did Karna first take the field at Kurukshetra?
options: On the first day, fighting beside Duryodhana
Only after Bhishma fell on the tenth day
On the thirteenth day, at the wheel formation
On the sixteenth day, as supreme commander
questions.explanation: Slighted by Bhishma, Karna refused to fight under the grandsire and entered the war only on the eleventh day.

questions.q: Who served, reluctantly, as Karna's charioteer in his final battles?
options: Sanjaya, lent by the blind king
Kritavarma of the Yadavas
Shalya, the king of Madra
Vrishasena, his own son
questions.explanation: Shalya drove Karna's chariot but, keeping a secret promise to Yudhishthira, undermined his confidence with constant taunts.

questions.q: How did Karna die?
options: Pierced through the heart in single combat at dusk
Crushed when his chariot overturned in the melee
Struck down by a celestial dart returned from heaven
Beheaded by Arjuna's arrow while freeing his sunken wheel
questions.explanation: As Karna struggled to lift his earth-trapped wheel, Arjuna's Anjalika arrow severed his head at Krishna's urging.

questions.q: Who warned Karna in advance that Indra would come begging for his armor?
options: His father Surya, appearing in a dream
His guru Parashurama, in a vision
His foster father Adhiratha
A wandering sage at his morning worship
questions.explanation: Surya appeared in a dream to warn his son, but Karna refused to break his vow of never turning a supplicant away.

questions.q: What did Krishna offer Karna before the war when he revealed the secret of his birth?
options: Command of the Pandava armies for all eighteen days
Kingship as the eldest Pandava, with even Draupadi as his queen
Half of the kingdom of Indraprastha joined with Anga
Safe passage to Dwarka and lordship over the Yadavas
questions.explanation: In the Udyoga Parva Krishna offered Karna the throne as Kunti's firstborn, with the Pandavas and even Draupadi attending him, but Karna chose loyalty to Duryodhana.

questions.q: Why did Karna beg Krishna to keep his parentage secret from Yudhishthira?
options: So Kunti would be spared public shame before the elders
So Arjuna would not refuse to fight him in the war
So the dharma-bound king would not surrender the throne to him
So Duryodhana would never come to doubt his loyalty
questions.explanation: Karna knew the righteous Yudhishthira would hand him the kingdom as eldest brother, and he would then have to pass it to Duryodhana.

questions.q: The epithet Vaikartana refers to which act of Karna's?
options: Conquering the four directions for Duryodhana
Holding the line of chariots at the Kaurava rear
Vowing at sunrise never to refuse a request
Cutting the armor from his own living flesh
questions.explanation: Karna earned the name Vaikartana by slicing off his birth-armor for Indra, though the word also marks him as the son of Surya, called Vikartana.

questions.q: During the final duel, the snake Ashwasena hid in Karna's arrow seeking revenge on Arjuna. What did Karna do when that arrow missed?
options: He refused to shoot the same arrow twice, despite the snake's plea
He blessed the snake and aimed it at Krishna instead
He cast the snake into the earth with a counter-spell
He saved the arrow for the final hour of the duel
questions.explanation: True to his warrior's pride, Karna declared he never loosed the same arrow twice, and the snake departed unavenged.

questions.q: Which son of Karna was slain by Arjuna in battle shortly before Karna's own death?
options: Vrishaketu
Vrishasena
Banasena
Chitrasena
questions.explanation: Arjuna killed Vrishasena before his father's eyes on the seventeenth day, answering the slaughter of Abhimanyu.

questions.q: How did Bhishma rate Karna when ranking the warriors before the war, prompting Karna to sit out?
options: As a maharatha equal to Arjuna himself
As unfit to fight in any battle formation
As only an ardha-ratha, half a chariot-warrior
As a bowman fit merely to guard the supply lines
questions.explanation: Bhishma's dismissive rating of Karna as an ardha-ratha so insulted him that he refused to fight while the grandsire commanded.

questions.q: What did Yudhishthira do upon learning, after the war, that Karna had been his eldest brother?
options: He cursed all women to be unable to keep secrets
He renounced the throne and left for the forest at once
He ordered a golden statue of Karna raised at court
He vowed never to touch dice or wagers again
questions.explanation: Grief-stricken that Kunti had hidden the truth, Yudhishthira cursed womankind that no secret would ever stay hidden in their hearts.

questions.q: According to the epic, where did the basket carrying the infant Karna finally come to rest?
options: At Hastinapura's bathing ghats on the Ganga
In the reeds of the Yamuna near Mathura
At Champa, where the charioteer Adhiratha found him
On the banks of the Saraswati at Prabhasa
questions.explanation: The basket drifted from the Ashva river down to the Ganga and reached Champa, the city where Adhiratha and Radha lived.
